fe-5-document-write: Shows how JavaScript can reveal a hidden element. A paragraph starts with `display:none`; clicking the button sets `style.display='block'` on `#demo` through an `onclick` handler and `getElementById`. Click **Run**, then press **Click Me!** in the preview.
fe-5-window-alert: Demonstrates `window.alert()` with a numeric expression. `alert(5 + 6)` evaluates to `11` before the dialog appears. The page loads behind the dialog — dismiss the alert to see it.
fe-5-external-script: Loads code from an external file via `<script src="myjs.js">` and also runs inline `document.write()` during page parse. `myjs.js` writes first, then the inline script injects an `<h1>` and extra text into the preview.
fe-5-date-on-click: A button `onclick` handler updates two elements: `#demo` receives the current `Date()` and `#demo2` receives fixed text, using `getElementById` and `innerHTML`. Click **Run**, then press the button in the preview.
fe-5-change-image: Toggles a bulb image on and off by changing the `src` attribute with `getElementById('myImage').src`, switching between `pic_bulbon.gif` and `pic_bulboff.gif`. Use the on/off buttons in the preview after **Run**.
fe-5-change-style: Function `myfunction(buttonId)` enlarges `#demo` with `style.fontSize='35px'`, then alerts the clicked button's label via `getElementById(buttonId).innerText`. Click button **1** in the preview after **Run**.
fe-5-hide-element: Hides a paragraph when the button is clicked by setting `style.display='none'` on `#demo`. Click **Run**, then press **Click Me!** in the preview.
fe-5-show-element: Demonstrates string concatenation: `document.write("5" + 6)` outputs `56` because the number is coerced to a string. Also calls `console.log("Hello")` — open the **Console** tab after **Run** to see that line.
fe-5-global-variable: Declares a global `var myvariable = 5` and logs it through `window`, `self`, and `globalThis`. `myfunction()` declares a local `myvariable = 9` and alerts it, demonstrating shadowing. The page writes the global value `5`; open the **Console** tab for the log lines.
fe-5-objects: Creates an object literal with numeric keys `1` and `2` plus property `eId`. Reads values with bracket notation (`employee[1]`) and dot notation (`employee.eId`), then writes them to the page with `document.write`.
fe-5-variable-scope: `var z` is declared inside an `if` block within `myfunction()`, but that function is never called. `console.log(z)` in the outer scope throws `ReferenceError: z is not defined`, which is caught and written to the page. Open the **Console** tab to see the error there as well.
fe-5-dialogs: Defines `showmsg()` which calls `window.prompt()` for user input and writes the result with `document.writeln`. The body script also writes the global `myvalue` (`60`). The prompt appears on load — the function's local `myvalue` shadows the global.
fe-6-string-trim: Declares a padded string and shows before/after values in an alert: `e.trim()` removes leading and trailing whitespace. Dismiss the alert after **Run** to see the page heading.
fe-6-array-isarray: Creates a fruit array and writes `Array.isArray(fruits)` to the page. Returns `true` for a real array and would return `false` for a non-array value.
fe-6-foreach: `numbers.forEach(myFunction)` concatenates each value into `txt` and calls `document.write(txt)` on every iteration, so the written output grows: `45`, then `454`, then `4549`, and so on.
fe-6-map: `Array.map()` doubles each element with `value * 2` and writes both the original array and the mapped result to the page.
fe-6-filter: `Array.filter()` keeps values greater than 18 from `[45, 4, 9, 16, 25]`, producing `45,25` on the page.
fe-6-reduceright: `Array.reduceRight()` folds the array from right to left: `25 - 16 - 9 - 4 - 45`, which evaluates to `-49`.
fe-6-every-some: `Array.some()` checks whether any element exceeds 18 and writes `true`. Commented code in the file shows the related `every()` method for comparison.
fe-6-find: `Array.find()` returns the first element greater than 18 — here `25` from `[4, 9, 16, 25, 29]`.
fe-6-json-stringify: Converts a JavaScript object to a JSON string with `JSON.stringify()` and writes it to the page with `document.writeln`, producing `{"name":"John","age":30,"city":"New York"}`.
fe-6-filter-arrow: Filters values greater than 18 twice: once with an ES5 named callback and once with an ES6 arrow function `(value) => value > 18`. Both write `45,25` to the page.
fe-6-flatmap: `[1, 2, 3, 4].flatMap(x => [x, x * 2])` maps each item to a pair then flattens one level into `[1, 2, 2, 4, 3, 6, 4, 8]`. The array is written to the page and logged — open the **Console** tab for the `console.log` output.
fe-6-padstart: `String.padStart(7, '0')` left-pads `'1699'` to seven characters (`'0001699'`). A second call uses space padding. Each result and its `.length` are written to the page.
fe-6-promise-basic: Models a pizza order with a `Promise` and a 3-second `setTimeout`. `pizzaReady` is `false`, so the promise rejects and `.catch()` logs the error. Status lines appear in the preview and in the **Console** tab — wait ~3 seconds after **Run**.
fe-6-promise-then: Creates a `Promise` that resolves after 3 seconds, then uses `.then()` to set `#demo` innerHTML to `I love You !!`. The result appears in the preview after the delay.
fe-6-async-await: `async function dinnerTime()` awaits `orderPizza()` (5 s) while a `setInterval` logs movie progress every second. Progress appears in the preview and **Console** tab; wait for the full sequence after **Run**.
